title: Healthy Herby Courgette Fritters
summary: One of the best ways to nourish your gut microbiome is to eat a wide range of plants and these courgette fritters are an easy, delicious way to do that. We’ve served them with a probiotic-rich tahini yoghurt dipping sauce.

method.description: Grate the courgettes and get them in a sieve over a bowl with a pinch of salt. Set aside.
Thinly slice the spring onion, chilli, basil and parsley.
Squeeze out the liquid from the courgettes and add to a mixing bowl, along with the herbs and spring onion.

Sprinkle the chickpea flour in with the courgettes, a couple of tablespoons at a time, and mix together until it forms a thick, spoonable batter (you might need slightly more or less than listed depending on how watery the veg is).
Combine the dipping sauce ingredients in a bowl and season with salt.
Heat a glug of olive oil in a frying pan over a medium heat and then add a spoonful of the courgette batter, flattening it slightly with a spoon.
Fry for a couple of minutes on each side until crisp and golden. Serve with the dipping sauce.

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
